etymology.description: semantic 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 羊(羊) (yáng) -> meaning
etymology.interpretation: The character 洋(yáng, "ocean; foreign") combines 氵(shuǐ, "water") as the semantic component, indicating a connection to water or large bodies of water such as the ocean. The phonetic component 羊(yáng) lends its sound to the character. The expansive and vast nature of the ocean extends to the concept of 'foreign', mirroring the idea of distant or overseas places.

examples.chinese: 阳光洒在海洋上(陽光灑在海洋上)。
他喜欢洋食(他喜歡洋食)，所以经常去外国餐厅(所以經常去外國餐廳)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān yángshí, suǒyǐ jīngcháng qù wàiguó cāntīng.
examples.english: He likes foreign food, so he often goes to international restaurants.

examples.chinese: 洋溢着欢笑的气氛(洋溢著歡笑的氣氛)充满了整个房间(充滿了整個房間)。
examples.pinyin: Yángyìzhe huānxiào de qìfēn chōngmǎnle zhěnggè fángjiān.
examples.english: An atmosphere filled with laughter pervaded the entire room.
